skill.name: uniprot-database
skill.description: Use UniProt REST API guidance and a Python helper to search proteins, retrieve FASTA records, map identifiers, and choose response fields.
skill.summary: Query UniProt protein records, sequences, annotations, and ID mappings through REST API workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: AI review found no malicious intent or prompt injection. The high static findings are false positives from markdown backticks and biology terms, but the skill includes a networked Python client and examples that can write downloaded data locally.

medium_findings.title: Network Client Lacks Explicit Timeouts
medium_findings.description: The helper script makes UniProt REST requests and polls mapping jobs without request timeouts. This is legitimate functionality, but production users should add timeouts and retry limits to avoid indefinite waits or stalled workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly calls requests.get and requests.post and uses an open polling loop. The behavior is intended for UniProt access, but the availability risk is clear.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Outbound UniProt API Access
low_findings.description: Network findings are true positives because the skill calls public UniProt REST endpoints. The destination is consistent with the skill purpose, and no evidence of credential exfiltration or unrelated domains was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The base URL and documentation endpoints point to rest.uniprot.org. The network activity matches the declared UniProt database purpose.

low_findings.title: Local File Writes in Download Examples
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings are true positives in documentation examples that save FASTA downloads. They write user-facing output files and do not target sensitive paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples use open() with filenames like human_proteins.fasta or user-supplied output_file. No overwrite of system files or secret collection is evident.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections are caused by markdown code fences, inline code, and curl examples. I found no Ruby backtick execution or runtime shell invocation in the skill script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown examples or literal query syntax. The Python script imports requests and argparse, but no subprocess, os.system, eval, or shell backtick execution appears.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Findings Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are not supported by code context. The cited areas contain descriptions, biology terms, or API field names, with no cryptographic library use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not call MD5, SHA1, DES, or any crypto function. The scanner appears to have matched substrings in normal documentation.

content.value_statement: Protein researchers need accurate UniProt queries without memorizing REST endpoints. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through searches, FASTA retrieval, ID mapping, and field selection.
actual_capabilities: Builds UniProtKB search queries using names, accessions, organisms, GO terms, ranges, and review status.
Retrieves individual protein entries in JSON, FASTA, TSV, XML, TXT, RDF, and related formats.
Maps identifiers between UniProtKB, PDB, Ensembl, RefSeq, GO, KEGG, and other supported databases.
Explains field selection for smaller UniProt responses, including sequence, annotation, feature, and cross-reference fields.
Provides a Python REST client for search, retrieval, batch retrieval, streaming, and ID mapping workflows.
Shows Python, curl, R, and JavaScript examples for common UniProt API operations.
limitations: Requires internet access to UniProt endpoints and depends on UniProt service availability.
Does not validate biological conclusions or replace expert review of annotations.
Large streaming queries can download substantial data and may need local storage planning.
The helper script does not include authentication, retry policy, or request timeout configuration.

use_cases.title: Find reviewed protein records
use_cases.description: Search Swiss-Prot entries for a gene, organism, disease term, or protein family before downstream analysis.

use_cases.title: Convert protein identifiers
use_cases.description: Map UniProt accessions to PDB, Ensembl, RefSeq, GO, KEGG, or other database identifiers.

use_cases.title: Prepare sequence datasets
use_cases.description: Retrieve FASTA or TSV datasets with selected fields for sequence analysis and annotation pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Search for one protein
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the UniProt database skill to find reviewed human entries for insulin. Return accessions, protein names, genes, and sequence lengths.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve FASTA records
prompt_templates.prompt: Use UniProt REST guidance to retrieve FASTA sequences for P01308 and P04637. Explain the endpoint format and expected result.

prompt_templates.title: Map identifiers across databases
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the UniProt ID mapping workflow to map UniProt accessions P01308 and P04637 to PDB identifiers. Include job submission and result retrieval steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a targeted protein query
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a UniProt query for reviewed human kinases with PDB structures. Select TSV fields for accession, gene, protein name, length, and function.

output_examples.input: Find reviewed human BRCA1 records with useful annotation fields.
output_examples.output: A concise UniProt query, recommended TSV fields, and a summary table plan with accession, gene name, organism, length, and function.

output_examples.input: Map several UniProt accessions to PDB identifiers.
output_examples.output: A three-step ID mapping workflow that submits the job, checks status, and retrieves mapped database identifiers.

output_examples.input: Download reviewed human proteins as FASTA.
output_examples.output: A stream endpoint recommendation, storage cautions for large results, and guidance to process chunks instead of loading all data.

best_practices: Filter with reviewed:true when curated Swiss-Prot entries are required.
Request only needed fields to reduce response size and processing time.
Use streaming and batching carefully for large result sets.
anti_patterns: Do not treat TrEMBL predictions as manually reviewed evidence.
Do not request every field when a small TSV result is enough.
Do not run large downloads without estimating size and storage impact.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps users query UniProt protein data through REST API examples, query syntax, field references, and a Python helper.

faq.question: Does it support FASTA retrieval?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents single-entry FASTA retrieval and streaming FASTA downloads for larger query results.

faq.question: Can it map UniProt IDs to other databases?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers the UniProt ID mapping job workflow and lists many supported source and target databases.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: No API key is documented. The included examples use public UniProt REST endpoints.

faq.question: Can it analyze biological meaning automatically?
faq.answer: No. It retrieves and organizes UniProt data, but users must validate scientific interpretation.

faq.question: Is it safe for large batch jobs?
faq.answer: It can support large jobs, but users should add timeouts, rate limits, and storage controls for production work.
